techniques.evidence_quote: the adversary leveraged CVE-2021-35464 to exploit a ForgeRock OpenAM application server
social engineering, where the adversary leveraged phone calls, SMS and/or Telegram to impersonate IT staff
the adversary leveraged compromised credentials from a victim user and authenticated to the organization's Azure tenant
the adversary used elevated privileges to execute the open-source LINpeas privilege escalation utility
the adversary executed the open-source port scanner tool RustScan from a Docker container running on an ESXi appliance
Leveraging AWS Instance Roles to assume or elevate privileges from the Apache Tomcat user
request and assume permissions of an instance role using a compromised AWS token
request and assume permissions of an instance role using a compromised AWS token
leveraging MFA push-notification fatigue. This is when an adversary continuously prompts MFA to the victim user until they accept the MFA push challenge
the adversary gained access to the organization's MFA console to add their own devices (as an additional device per user) as trusted MFA devices
directing victims to a credential harvesting site
add their own devices (as an additional device per user) as trusted MFA devices
create temporary federated credentials for non-existent users issued by identity and access management (IAM) users
the adversary used a wide variety of RMM tools to maintain persistent access
Federated Credentials help obfuscate which AWS credential is compromised and enables the adversary to pivot from the AWS CLI to console sessions without the need for MFA
enables the adversary to pivot from the AWS CLI to console sessions without the need for MFA
the adversary attempted to bypass AV or EDR security tools on the endpoints
the adversary accessed Azure Active Directory and performed bulk downloads of group members and users
the adversary executed the open-source port scanner tool RustScan
the adversary showcased their knowledge of AWS
lateral movement via Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) using Impacket
SSH tunneling and various remote access tools
accessed SharePoint and OneDrive environments for reconnaissance information
SSH RevShell and RDP Tunnelling via SSH
the adversary installed the open-source rsocx reverse proxy tool
the adversary downloaded tools using victim organization systems from sites such as file[.]io, GitHub, and paste[.]ee
The site transfer[.]sh was used by the adversary to perform data exfiltration of reconnaissance information
